skill.name: frontend-developer
skill.description: Build React and Next.js interfaces with responsive layouts, accessible components, client-side state, and frontend performance guidance.
skill.summary: Create production-ready React and Next.js frontends with accessibility and performance guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and were false positives from prose about frontend development. No executable code, cryptographic implementation, reconnaissance command, network request, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The flagged lines describe frontend topics such as client-side state, design systems, CSS, accessibility, and TypeScript examples. No weak cryptographic algorithm, hashing call, key handling, or executable cryptography code appears.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown guidance and the cited lines contain ordinary frontend terminology. There is no code path that invokes MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic API.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The flagged system and network reconnaissance lines refer to CSS Grid, Flexbox, Playwright, and Cypress. No command, scanner, host enumeration, port probing, or external network access appears.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is descriptive prose about layout and browser testing tools. It contains no shell command, network scan target, or runtime behavior.

content.value_statement: Modern frontend work needs fast, accessible, and maintainable interfaces. This skill guides React and Next.js implementation from component structure to performance validation.
actual_capabilities: Plans React and Next.js component architecture for production applications.
Guides responsive layouts using modern CSS and design system patterns.
Recommends client state, server state, and data fetching approaches.
Improves accessibility with semantic HTML, ARIA, and keyboard behavior.
Optimizes rendering, bundles, images, and Core Web Vitals.
Suggests frontend tests using React Testing Library, Playwright, and Storybook.
limitations: It provides guidance and generated code, not independent runtime verification.
It does not replace product design research or visual brand direction.
It assumes a web frontend stack, mainly React and Next.js.
It may need project files to match local conventions accurately.

use_cases.title: Build a React Feature
use_cases.description: Create accessible components, state handling, and loading states for a new product workflow.

use_cases.title: Improve Next.js Performance
use_cases.description: Review rendering choices, bundle size, image handling, and Core Web Vitals opportunities.

use_cases.title: Standardize UI Patterns
use_cases.description: Define reusable component patterns that fit an existing design system and testing workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a responsive React component for this UI. Include accessibility states, TypeScript props, and basic tests.

prompt_templates.title: Fix a Frontend Issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this frontend issue and propose a fix. Consider layout, state, accessibility, and browser behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Next.js Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Design the frontend architecture for this Next.js feature. Cover routing, data fetching, loading states, and error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a Complex App
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this React or Next.js flow for performance. Recommend changes for rendering, caching, bundle size, and Core Web Vitals.

output_examples.input: Create an accessible product card grid for a commerce page.
output_examples.output: A component plan with responsive grid behavior, semantic structure, image handling, keyboard focus rules, and test coverage notes.

output_examples.input: Improve a slow dashboard built with React Query.
output_examples.output: A performance review covering query boundaries, memoization, skeleton loading, virtualization, and rendering bottlenecks.

output_examples.input: Plan authentication pages in a Next.js App Router project.
output_examples.output: A routing and component strategy with form validation, server action boundaries, error states, and accessibility requirements.

best_practices: Provide existing framework, styling, and testing conventions before requesting implementation.
Ask for accessibility and performance acceptance criteria with each frontend task.
Review generated code against the local design system before merging.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for backend-only API design without frontend scope.
Do not request broad rewrites when a focused component fix is enough.
Do not skip manual browser testing for complex responsive behavior.

faq.question: Does this skill support React 19?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes React 19 patterns, including Actions, Server Components, Suspense, and concurrent rendering guidance.

faq.question: Can it help with Next.js App Router?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers App Router structure, Server Components, streaming, route handlers, middleware, and rendering choices.

faq.question: Does it generate complete production code?
faq.answer: It can generate implementation guidance and code, but users should test the result in their project.

faq.question: Can it improve accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It emphasizes semantic HTML, ARIA patterns, keyboard navigation, focus management, and WCAG guidance.

faq.question: Is it useful for design systems?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help define reusable components, tokens, theming, documentation, and Storybook usage.

faq.question: Does it perform network or system actions?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The skill is Markdown guidance and contains no executable scripts or network calls.
